user_query:
I'm preparing a classroom handout on "Whigs vs. Democrats in Antebellum America" and need it to be high-quality, structured, and classroom-appropriate. Please use the provided notes and excerpts to produce a complete handout and supporting artifacts, and validate the handout with the included linter.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/notes/antebellum_whig_dem_notes.csv
- input/primary/excerpts.jsonl
- input/draft/handout_template.md
- input/draft/email_to_parents.txt
- input/checks/handout_lint.py

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Build the handout and outline
- Create build/outline.json with this structure:
  {
    "title": string,
    "overview": string,
    "key_themes": [ {"id": string, "theme": string, "point": string} ],
    "primary_excerpts": [ {"id": string, "source": string, "year": number, "excerpt": string} ],
    "discussion_questions": [ string, ... ],
    "further_reading": [ string, ... ]
  }
  Requirements:
  - Use the notes and excerpts from the input files; include their ids so usage can be cross-checked.
  - Overview should be 1–2 short paragraphs.
  - Include at least 4 items in key_themes, at least 2 primary_excerpts, at least 3 discussion_questions, and at least 1 further_reading item.
- Create build/handout.md that reflects the same content as outline.json and is organized with the following section headings in this exact order:
  ## Overview
  ## Key Themes
  ## Primary Source Excerpts
  ## Discussion Questions
  ## Further Reading
  Formatting expectations for the linter:
  - In "Primary Source Excerpts", include at least two blockquoted lines (start each excerpt line with "> ").
  - In "Discussion Questions", include at least three questions, each starting with "- ".
  - In "Further Reading", include at least one "- " bullet item.
  - Avoid flagged partisan terms (the linter checks for: corrupt, evil, traitor, un-American; case-insensitive) in the handout.

2) Run the linter and iterate based on its output
- First, run the linter on the unmodified template to capture baseline issues:
  python input/checks/handout_lint.py input/draft/handout_template.md
  Save the exact console output to build/logs/lint_before.txt.
- Then run the linter on your completed build/handout.md and revise the handout as needed until there are no ERROR lines and it reports success. Save the final linter run output to build/logs/lint_after.txt.
- Also write build/lint_summary.json summarizing the results as:
  {"errors_before": <int>, "errors_after": <int>, "passed": <true|false>}.
  Set passed to true only if the final linter run prints "OK: No issues found.".

3) Rewrite the parent email for tone, clarity, and brevity
- Using input/draft/email_to_parents.txt, produce two files:
  - build/email_neutral.txt: a neutral, classroom-appropriate rewrite preserving all logistical details (dates/times/deadlines) but removing loaded/partisan language and improving clarity.
  - build/email_brief.txt: a concise version under 120 words that still clearly communicates the schedule and expectations.

Notes:
- Analyze and act on the linter’s command output and any error messages; do not ignore them.
- Ensure the content is accurate, balanced for a classroom (I’m a Whig sympathizer, but the handout should present perspectives fairly), and that the final handout passes the linter.
- All outputs should be written to the build/ directory paths specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
